details: The agent ran comprehensive kubectl commands against the cluster and correctly identified all 11 fault namespaces with accurate root-cause categories: (1) fault-configmap: missing ConfigMap 'app-config'; (2) fault-crashloop: application crash due to DB connection refused; (3) fault-endpoints: service selector mismatch (app=web-backend-v2 vs app=web-backend); (4) fault-imagepull: non-existent image nginx:1.99.99-nonexistent; (5) fault-init: init container crash due to locked migration table; (6) fault-multicontainer: sidecar crash due to DNS resolution failure; (7) fault-oom: OOMKilled with 25Mi memory limit; (8) fault-pvc: missing StorageClass 'fast-ssd'; (9) fault-readiness: readiness probe connection refused on port 8080; (10) fault-rollout: stuck rollout due to bad image nginx:2.0-does-not-exist; (11) fault-unschedulable: node affinity/selector mismatch. The healthy namespace was not flagged. All diagnoses are supported by actual command output evidence.
